Body:
```markdown
## Related Issues

- Addresses #84141.
- Related: #60841.

## Summary

- Enables the Codex app-server native/dynamic tool surface for finite cron allowlists that require runtime tools like `exec`.
- Fails cron isolated-agent runs closed with `TOOL_SURFACE_UNAVAILABLE` when declared required tools are not available before model dispatch.
- Hardens cron finalization so success-shaped summaries that mention missing required tools are treated as failures, not healthy cron output.

## Root Cause

Cron `payload.toolsAllow` was being treated as an allowlist/filter, not as a required pre-dispatch capability contract. In the Codex app-server path, finite allowlists such as `["exec", "read"]` disabled the native app-server tool surface, while the default dynamic filter also removed Codex-owned tools like `exec`. That combination could produce empty/no-exec tool surfaces and still allow a model turn to produce operator-facing “exec unavailable” text.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron isolated-agent jobs that declare finite required tools, especially `toolsAllow: ["exec", "read"]`, must either expose the required OpenClaw dynamic tool surface before model dispatch or fail closed with `TOOL_SURFACE_UNAVAILABLE`. They must not produce success-shaped health summaries when no exec/shell tool is available.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at `/Users/ostemini/projects/openclaw-cron-tool-surface-mainline-20260520`, branch `codex/cron-tool-surface-fail-closed-20260520`, rebased onto `origin/main` commit `6c7fe58468`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`. The live affected OpenClaw install that motivated the patch was OpenClaw `2026.5.18` on the same host; it was inspected but not updated by this PR.

**Before evidence:** Copied live output from the affected host before installing this source patch showed valid exec-required cron payloads but an empty/no-exec Codex app-server tool surface:

```text
Runtime Health Guard payload.toolsAllow: ["exec", "read"]
codexDynamicToolsExclude: ["read", "write", "edit", "apply_patch", "process", "update_plan"]
runtime-guard issues included: "cron_tool_surface_false_ok:9"
runtime-guard issues included: "codex_app_tools_exec_unavailable:empty_manifest"
codexAppToolsCacheToolCount: 0
codexAppToolsCacheHasExec: false
```

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** In the patched source checkout, I ran:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-a

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

Changes span extensions/codex app-server tool surface logic and cron isolated-agent helpers.

per_label_rationale.cron_automation: The fix targets cron isolated-agent jobs and cron finalization behavior for scheduled runs.
per_label_rationale.exec_tools: Central issue is required exec/read tool allowlists and ensuring shell/exec tools are available before dispatch.
per_label_rationale.reliability: Adds fail-closed preflight and failure classification to avoid false healthy cron summaries when required tools are missing.

excluded_label_rationale.tool_calling: Although tool availability is involved, the change is about exec tool surfaces and allowlists, not model tool-call delta/schema/result behavior.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #75657: fix: local GGUF embedding model warmup blocks Node.js event loop for minutes on startup (ARM64/Pi)
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 75657
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75657
- Title: fix: local GGUF embedding model warmup blocks Node.js event loop for minutes on startup (ARM64/Pi)
- State: OPEN
- Author: DerFlash

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When `memorySearch.provider` is set to `"local"` (the default), the Gateway initializes a GGUF embedding model via node-llama-cpp on the main Node.js thread during startup. On ARM64 hardware (Raspberry Pi), loading a 314 MB GGUF model blocks the event loop for **approximately 6 minutes**, during which the Gateway is completely unreachable — even though it reports `active (running)` in systemd.

## Environment

- OS: Raspberry Pi, Linux 6.12.75+rpt-rpi-v8, arm64/aarch64
- Node.js: v24.14.1
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.29 (a448042)
- memorySearch config: `provider: "local"`, model: `hf:ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300m-qat-q8_0-GGUF/embeddinggemma-300m-qat-Q8_0.gguf` (314 MB)
- Gateway mode: systemd user service

## Observed behavior

Gateway systemd service starts, but the WebSocket port is not usable for ~6 minutes:

```
14:58:07  systemd: Started openclaw-gateway.service
14:58:22  [gateway] loading configuration…        ← 15s just to load config
14:58:25  [gateway] starting...
             ... silence for ~6 minutes ...
15:04:04  [gateway] startup model warmup timed out after 5000ms; continuing without waiting
15:04:04  [diagnostic] liveness warning:
            reasons=event_loop_delay,event_loop_utilization,cpu
            eventLoopDelayP99Ms=18555.6
            eventLoopDelayMaxMs=18555.6            ← 18.5 second event loop block
            eventLoopUtilization=1
            cpuCoreRatio=1.05
15:04:04  [telegram] [default] starting provider
15:04:04  [gateway] ready
```

**Time from service start to `gateway ready`: ~6 minutes.**

During this window:
- `openclaw status` reports gateway as "unreachable (timeout)"
- `openclaw tui` fails with "gateway not reachable"
- `openclaw logs --follow` fails immediately
- Telegram receives no responses
- All WebSocket connections time out

The `startup model warmup timed out after 5000ms` message confirms the Gateway itself has a warmup timeout, but the model initialization continues blocking the main thread regardless.

## Additional context

- CPU usage holds at ~40–60% throughout the warmup window (confirmed via `ps`)
- RSS reaches ~700 MB on a Pi with 4 GB RAM during warmup
- The `lsof` on the gateway PID shows fd/1 and fd/2 both point to a socket (systemd journal), not a file — ruling out log-file I/O as the cause
- Manually testing `import('...node_modules/json5/...')` from the dist directory succeeds instantly, confirming earlier `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors during restarts were a race condition symptom of th

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

The related open PRs cover prewarm or diagnostic-budget changes, not the needed worker or child-process isolation.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible rather than live-reproduced: configure explicit local memory embeddings and trigger deep status, memory index/sync, or the first `memory_search`; current main still enters the in-process `node-llama-cpp` provider path. I did not live-measure the ARM64/Pi startup timing.

**Next step**
This is a concrete source-reproducible runtime bug with clear likely files and no required config or product-policy change; the repair should focus on local embedding isolation, not startup prewarm.

Best possible solution:

Yes, source-reproducible rather than live-reproduced: configure explicit local memory embeddings and trigger deep status, memory index/sync, or the

The startup warmup timeout fires after 5s but model initialization continues blocking the main thread. A worker-thread or child-process isolation fix is needed.
ds4_caveats: Not live-reproduced on ARM64/Pi hardware; source-reproducible via explicit local memory embeddings config
title: fix: local GGUF embedding model warmup blocks Node.js event loop for minutes on startup (ARM64/Pi)

per_label_rationale.local_models: A local GGUF embedding model loaded via node-llama-cpp on ARM64/Pi is the central cause.
per_label_rationale.memory: The failing path is `memorySearch.provider: "local"` and local memory embeddings initialization/search.
per_label_rationale.reliability: The issue is a startup/event-loop blocking reliability failure causing timeouts and liveness warnings.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #45200: fix(subagents): notify user on announce give-up instead of silently dropping result
github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 45200 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/45200 - Title: fix(subagents): notify user on announce give-up instead of silently dropping result - State: OPEN - Author: waliddafif - Labels: agents, size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, mantis: telegram-visible-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 1 - Changed files: src/agents/subagent-registry.ts Body: ```markdown ## Problem When the subagent announce retry budget (`MAX_ANNOUNCE_RETRY_COUNT = 3`) is exhausted, `logAnnounceGiveUp` logs a warning, marks the run done, and the completed result is **permanently discarded with no indication to the user**. This is Pattern 1 from #44925 — documented across 9+ production incidents (E31, E42, E45, E79, E42 × n...): ``` Subagent completion direct announce failed Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) Subagent orphan run pruned ... reason=missing-session-entry ``` The user has no idea the task completed. They wait indefinitely or manually ask "what happened?". ## Fix Add `notifyAnnounceGiveUp(entry)`, called fire-and-forget from the `retry-limit` branch in `resumeSubagentRun()`. When `entry.requesterOrigin` carries a deliverable `channel` + `to` address (captured at spawn time, already stored in `SubagentRunRecord`), it calls the gateway `agent` method with `deliver: true` to push the result **directly to the user's channel**, bypassing the session-based announce pipeline that just failed. The notification surfaces: - a clear warning that delivery retried and failed - the task label - the recovered result text from `entry.frozenResultText` (when available) ## Behaviour | Scenario | Before | After | |---|---|---| | announce retry-limit hit, origin has channel+to | silent drop | best-effort last notify to user | | announce retry-limit hit, no usable origin | silent drop | silent drop (unchanged) | | last-resort notify also fails | silent drop | silent drop + audit log entry | Failure is intentionally swallowed — if the last-resort delivery also fails, the outcome is identical to the previous behaviour but the attempt is auditable. ## Scope This targets Pattern 1 (silent drop after retry-limit). Pattern 2 (timeout with no auto-restart) and Pattern 3 (file creation claims unverified) are separate issues. Related: #44925, #44919, #43367 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-13T14:55:11Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR addresses a real production pain point: when a subagent's announce retry budget is exhausted, the completed result was silently discarded with no user feedback. The fix adds a best-effort last-resort notifier (`notifyAnnounceGiveUp`) that, when the requester's delivery context is available, fires a one-shot gateway call to push a failure notice directly to the user's channel. Key observations: - **Missing `idempotencyKey`** — The `callGateway` params in `notifyAnnounceGiveUp` omit `idempotencyKey`, which the gateway `agent` handler assigns to both its deduplication key and `runId`. Without it, all concurrent give-up notifications share the same dedupe entry (`"agent:undefined"`), meaning a second concurrent call may be silently dropped, and `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` receives `undefined` as `runId`, risking downstream failures. - **Indirect delivery via LLM session** — The implementation routes `notifyText` as a user message through the requester agent's session rather than delivering it verbatim. The user receives the LLM's response to the notification. In the dominant failure scenario (`missing-session-entry`), the session is reconstructed from scratch with no history, so the LLM processes the notification out of context. - No new tests cover `notifyAnnounceGiveUp` or the `retry-limit` branch that invokes it.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- The PR is not safe to merge as-is due to the missin [comments/context truncated after 1500 chars] ``` Diff/context: ```diff carries a deliverable channel + to address, it calls the gateway agent channel, bypassing the session-based announce pipeline that just failed. --- diff --git a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts --- a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts +++ b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts @@ -16,6 +16,7 @@ import { onAgentEvent } from "../infra/agent-events.js"; @@ -132,6 +133,76 @@ function logAnnounceGiveUp(entry: SubagentRunRecord, reason: "retry-limit" | "ex + * session* so the agent can summarise and forward the result. When that path + * calling the gateway `agent` method with `deliver: true` so the gateway + * bypasses the session queue and sends the raw text to the user's inbox. + await callGateway({ + sessionKey: entry.requesterSessionKey, + kind: "inter_session", + sourceSessionKey: entry.childSessionKey, @@ -608,6 +679,10 @@ function resumeSubagentRun(runId: string) { --- diff --git a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts --- a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts +++ b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts @@ -150,6 +150,10 @@ function logAnnounceGiveUp(entry: SubagentRunRecord, reason: "retry-limit" | "ex 1. Missing idempotencyKey — callGateway({method:'agent'}) without idempotencyKey causes the gateway to use 'agent:undefined' as the collide and the second is silently dropped. dispatchAgentRunFromGateway context — retry-limit runs have an existing session; missing-session-entry --- diff --git a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts --- a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts +++ b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts @@ -17,6 +17,7 @@ import { createSubsystemLogger } from "../logging/subsystem.js"; @@ -169,15 +170,26 @@ async function notifyAnnounceGiveUp(entry: SubagentRunRecord): Promise { + // session (missing-session-entry runs are pruned by the orphan path before + // that an absent key would produce in the gateway dedup layer). await callGateway({ @@ -193,6 +205,7 @@ async function notifyAnnounceGiveUp(entry: SubagentRunRecord): Promise { sourceSessionKey: entry.childSessionKey, --- diff --git a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts --- a/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts +++ b/src/agents/subagent-registry.ts @@ -1023,6 +1023,10 @@ async function finalizeSubagentCleanup( [diff selected/truncated after 5000 chars] ```
ds4_description: PR 45200 fixes a production pain point in the subagent announce pipeline: when the retry budget (MAX_ANNOUNCE_RETRY_COUNT=3) is exhausted, the completed result was silently dropped with no user indication. The fix adds a best-effort last-resort notifier (notifyAnnounceGiveUp) that, when the requester's delivery context is available, fires a gateway agent call with deliver:true to push a failure notice directly to the user's channel, bypassing the session queue. Targets Pattern 1 from #44925 (9+ production incidents).
ds4_caveats: Greptile notes missing idempotencyKey in callGateway params — all concurrent give-up notifications share dedupe key 'agent:undefined', risking silent drops on concurrent calls
No new tests cover notifyAnnounceGiveUp or the retry-limit branch; Greptile confidence score 2/5, flagged as not safe to merge as-is

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #71216: Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 71216
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/71216
- Title: Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`
- State: OPEN
- Author: newbrevity
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, impact:session-state, impact:security, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
# Proposed upstream issue — openclaw/openclaw

**Title:** Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`

---

## Context

Running OpenClaw v2026.4.22 on macOS (Mac Studio M4 Max) as a single-user local gateway at `127.0.0.1:18789`. Using it for a mixed cloud / local-Ollama setup with sensitivity-based routing.

The current schema covers auth, bind, tailscale, controlUi, `gateway.nodes.denyCommands`, and `session.dmScope`. Four adjacent capabilities are not in the schema and would meaningfully reduce wrapper / sidecar code.

## Asks

1. **`sandbox.mode`** — global sandbox mode for tool execution. At minimum two values: `main` (current behavior) and `non-main` (sensitive operations refuse outside an approval flow). Today `appServer.mode: guardian` exists but is Codex-plugin-scoped.

2. **`routing.rules`** — tag-based provider selection. Useful shape:
   ```json
   {
     "routing": {
       "defaults": { "provider": "anthropic" },
       "rules": [
         {
           "match": { "tags": ["sensitive", "personal-finance"] },
           "provider": "ollama",
           "host": "127.0.0.1:11434",
           "model": "llama3:70b"
         }
       ]
     }
   }
   ```
   Any skill/agent call carrying one of the tags routes to the matching provider. Today this has to be enforced by a skill-level library that reads a policy sidecar.

3. **`instances.*`** — multi-instance awareness in one config so multiple gateways on the same host (one per user) can be declared and validated together, with per-instance workspace + port + active/deferred state. Today each instance needs a separate config file and wrapper-enforced isolation.

4. **`gateway.nodes.denyPaths`** — symmetric with the existing `denyCommands` array. A list of absolute paths/prefixes the gateway refuses to read or write regardless of which tool is asking. Covers credentials dirs, SSH keys, secrets archives.

## Why it matters

Without these, the policy gets split across the config, a sidecar JSON file, and per-skill code. Any skill that forgets the guard silently falls back to the default provider or touches a denyPath. Moving the four capabilities into the gateway would let `openclaw doctor` validate them and the gateway enforce them at one chokepoint.

## Happy to help

Willing to draft schema PRs for (1) and (4) if the project agrees the shape. (2) and (3) have more design surface and probably benefit from maintainer direction first.

---

**Environment**

- 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

- PR/issue authors and users with repository write access can comment `@clawsweeper re-review` or `@clawsweeper re-run` on an open PR or issue to request a fresh review only.
- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.
- Fresh-review commands do not start repair, autofix, rebase, CI repair, or automerge.
- Maintainer-only repair and merge flows require explicit commands such as `@clawsweeper autofix`, `@clawsweeper automerge`, `@clawsweeper fix ci`, or `@clawsweeper address review`.
- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

ds4_description: Feature request proposing config schema additions for sandbox.mode, routing.rules, multi-instance awareness, and gateway.nodes.denyPaths to centralize policy enforcement in the gateway config. The author is willing to contribute schema PRs for sandbox and denyPaths.
ds4_caveats: Issue remains open with no fix PR; routing.rules and instances need maintainer direction before implementation
Some items (sandbox, denyPaths) are more clearly scoped than others (routing.rules, instances)
title: Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`

per_label_rationale.config: The issue explicitly requests new config schema fields: sandbox, routing.rules, instances, and gateway.nodes.denyPaths.
per_label_rationale.gateway: Requested fields are to be validated and enforced by the OpenClaw gateway, including multi-gateway instances and gateway node deny paths.
per_label_rationale.local_model_providers: routing.rules is for tag-based provider routing in a mixed cloud/local Ollama setup with host and model selection.
per_label_rationale.sandboxing: sandbox.mode is a requested global sandbox mode for tool execution and isolation behavior.
per_label_rationale.security: denyPaths is intended to block reads/writes to credentials, SSH keys, and secrets regardless of tool.

excluded_label_rationale.local_models: Ollama is mentioned, but the request is about provider routing configuration rather than local model execution behavior.

ambiguity.why_not_hard: The title and asks directly name config, gateway, sandbox, routing, and security controls, with only minor boundary risk around local model routing.

github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 70002 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/70002 - Title: ci: skip docs sync & translate-trigger workflows in forks - State: OPEN - Author: xudaiyanzi - Labels: size: XS, triage: risky-infra, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, merge-risk: 🚨 automation, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look, proof: 📸 screenshot - Changed file count available to wrapper: 2 - Changed files: .github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml, .github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml Body: ```markdown ## Summary - **Problem:** Two upstream-only workflows (`docs-sync-publish.yml`, `docs-translate-trigger-release.yml`) fail with `Authentication failed for 'https://github.com/openclaw/docs.git/'` on every push to `main` in any fork, because they rely on the `OPENCLAW_DOCS_SYNC_TOKEN` secret that only exists in `openclaw/openclaw`. - **Why it matters:** Creates a red ✗ on every normal fork-sync (`git fetch upstream main && git push origin main`), confuses new contributors, and spams fork Actions dashboards with irrecoverable failures. - **What changed:** Added `if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw'` to the single job in each of the two workflows — the same guard pattern already used ~30 times across the repo (`ci.yml`, `codeql.yml`, `control-ui-locale-refresh.yml`). - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to workflow logic, secrets, the publish repo, the sync script (`scripts/docs-sync-publish.mjs`), documentation, or any other workflow. Behavior in `openclaw/openclaw` is byte-for-byte identical. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [x] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) - **Root cause:** The two workflows were added without the fork-guard expression that the rest of the CI already uses. When the workflow runs in a fork, `${{ secrets.OPENCLAW_DOCS_SYNC_TOKEN }}` expands to an empty string, so the clone/push URL becomes `https://x-access-token:@github.com/openclaw/docs.git/` and GitHub rejects it with `Invalid username or token. Password authentication is not supported for Git operations.` - **Missing detection / guardrail:** No `if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw'` on the job, even though the same repository relies on that exact check in ~30 other places to keep fork runs safe. - **Contributing context:** The workflows were likely adapted from local-only automation; the push-to-external-repo step is what makes the fork-guard a requirement rather than a nicety. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integra [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-22T05:50:52Z:

Greptile Summary

Adds `if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw'` to the single job in `docs-sync-publish.yml` and `docs-translate-trigger-release.yml`, matching the fork-guard pattern already used ~30 times across the repo. This prevents authentication failures in forks where `OPENCLAW_DOCS_SYNC_TOKEN` is unavailable, with zero behavior change on the upstream repository.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — minimal, idiomatic change with no upstream behavior impact. Both changes are a single-line addition of a standard GitHub Actions job-level condition, consistent with the repo's existing guard pattern. No logic, secrets, or workflow behavior is altered for the upstream repository. No files require special attention. A couple of notes to make review easier: - The change is 2 lines total, both adding the same `if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw'` guard already used in `ci.yml`, `codeql.yml`, and `control-ui-locale-refresh.yml`. - No behavior change on the upstream repo; the guard only affects fork [comments/context truncated after 1500 chars] ``` Diff/context: ```diff From 1c4f9382034c7ac99db0418d51510012651265ee Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 From: xudaiyanzi Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 00:37:51 -0500 Subject: [PATCH] ci: skip docs sync & translate-trigger workflows in forks --- .github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml | 1 + .github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml | 1 + 2 files changed, 2 insertions(+) diff --git a/.github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml b/.github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml index df4ed519f305..5b5b12004476 100644 --- a/.github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/docs-sync-publish.yml @@ -15,6 +15,7 @@ permissions: jobs: sync-publish-repo: + if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw' runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - name: Checkout source repo diff --git a/.github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml b/.github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml index 677ed8181dfc..27c705d2eb59 100644 --- a/.github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/docs-translate-trigger-release.yml @@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ permissions: jobs: dispatch-translate: + if: github.repository == 'openclaw/openclaw' runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - name: Trigger locale translates in publish repo ```
